meta.title: Pre-Registered Replication of Solvent-Polarity Effects on a Diels–Alder Cycloaddition
meta.authors: F. Careful, G. Rigorous, H. Thorough

summary.headline: No confirmed flags. 2 dimensions routed to a human; 1 candidate flag self-retracted on fresh-context re-read.

dropped_flags.reason: A fresh-context confirmation pass (DESIGN §13) re-read the table and retracted this candidate flag. The first pass folded the '18 excluded after screening' footnote into the denominator and reported a 215 vs 233 mismatch. On a clean re-read the reported total (215) reconciles exactly with the two arm sizes (120 + 95); the 18 excluded are pre-enrollment and never entered the total. Recorded as a self-caught false positive rather than shown as a finding — the autonomy evidence.

finding.message: FIRST-PASS CANDIDATE (retracted): suspected the 'Total enrolled' did not match the per-arm sizes. Superseded by the fresh-context re-read below.

evidence.quote: A total of 215 participants were enrolled (arm A, n = 120; arm B, n = 95); 18 were excluded after screening.
evidence.recompute_script:
# T0 table-total verifier (FIRST PASS, later RETRACTED on fresh context).
# First pass mis-read the "n = 18 excluded" footnote as part of the denominator.
# On a clean re-read the reported total reconciles exactly with the arm sizes.

arm_a = 120          # Table 1, arm A enrolled
arm_b = 95           # Table 1, arm B enrolled
reported_total = 215 # Table 1, "Total enrolled"

computed_total = arm_a + arm_b
ok = computed_total == reported_total

print(f"arm A enrolled:   {arm_a}")
print(f"arm B enrolled:   {arm_b}")
print(f"sum of arms:      {computed_total}")
print(f"reported total:   {reported_total}")
print(f"VERDICT: PASS - reported total reconciles with the arm sizes" if ok else f"VERDICT: FAIL - off by {abs(computed_total - reported_total)}")
evidence.expected_output:
arm A enrolled:   120
arm B enrolled:   95
sum of arms:      215
reported total:   215
VERDICT: PASS - reported total reconciles with the arm sizes

routed_to_human.note: The introduction positions the work as the first systematic replication of this solvent-polarity series. Whether that constitutes a novel-enough contribution is a judgment for the field, not a verifiable proposition. Surfaced, not scored (DESIGN §3.5).
routed_to_human.quote: To our knowledge, this is the first pre-registered replication of the full solvent-polarity series.

routed_to_human.note: The Discussion argues the reconciled rate constants 'settle a long-standing disagreement' in the literature. Whether the disagreement is settled is a matter of expert significance-weighting; LITMUS routes it rather than rendering a verdict.
routed_to_human.quote: These results should settle the long-standing disagreement over the polarity dependence of this cycloaddition.
